headline: Digital Literacy Gap Leaves 45% of Rural Students Behind
context: MOE's 2025 digital readiness assessment found nearly half of rural students lack basic computer skills.

cards.big: 45% of rural students scored below basic on MOE digital literacy test 2025
cards.sub: Urban students outperform rural peers by 3.2 grade levels on average

cards.big: Only 54% of rural schools have functional computer labs as of 2025 audit
cards.sub: Maintenance budgets for ICT equipment have been flat since 2020

cards.big: MCMC's JENDELA project promised 98% 4G coverage by 2025 but reached 92%
cards.sub: Coverage gaps concentrate in Sabah, Sarawak, and Orang Asli areas

cards.big: Estonia achieved 95% student digital proficiency through mandatory coding from age 7
cards.sub: Malaysia introduced optional coding in 2020 with limited teacher training

cards.big: Digital exclusion in schools today becomes economic exclusion in workplaces tomorrow
Should digital literacy be a core subject equal to mathematics and science
